notes: Full graphify re-run 2026-07-11: ingested cognee-research.md, mex-research.md, palantir-foundry-ontology-research.md (previously missing from graph).
Skipped sources/README.md (workspace boilerplate) and sources/interview-template.md (empty template, not evidence).
Thirteen sources: five internal product-intent docs + eight external research artifacts. Still zero user-interview or support-ticket evidence.
Internal sources (prod-plan.md, plan.md, memory-wiki-plan.md, teamshared-readme.md, teamshared-agents.md) lack YAML frontmatter; types inferred as prd/memo/doc.
Cognee classified Tier-1 direct memory competitor alongside GBrain. mex Tier-3 adjacent. Palantir ontology = architectural reference only.
consent-first capture removed from product (2026-06-19); memory-wiki-plan.md retains historical consent design.

nodes.label: Multi-tenant organizations / teams

provenance.quote: The system should allow teams, companies, and agentic workflows to share structured and unstructured memory across users, agents, projects, and tools.

nodes.label: Enterprise buyers

provenance.quote: Enterprise security baseline
SSO/SAML/OIDC

nodes.label: Agents / agentic workflows (first-class identities)

provenance.quote: Agents and API keys must be first-class identities with scoped permissions.

nodes.label: Founders building 'company brain' products (YC RFS category)

provenance.quote: If you're building this, you should apply to YC.

nodes.label: Power users running personal autonomous agents (OpenClaw/Hermes)

provenance.quote: GBrain is designed to be installed and operated by an AI agent.

nodes.label: Teams shopping for a 'shared brain' across coding agents (Claude Code/Cursor/Codex/OpenClaw/Hermes)

provenance.quote: Multi-Agent Memory gives your AI agents a shared brain that works across machines, tools, and frameworks. Store a fact from Claude Code on your laptop, recall it from an OpenClaw agent on your server.

nodes.label: Share memory across users, agents, projects, and tools

provenance.quote: Think of TeamShared as infrastructure for "shared agent context" across an organization.

nodes.label: Persistent recall without overwhelming the context window

provenance.quote: give agents persistent, personalized recall without overwhelming their context windows

nodes.label: No cross-tenant memory leakage

provenance.quote: The system must prevent cross-tenant memory leakage by design, not only by convention.

nodes.label: Get synthesized answers with citations, not raw page lists

provenance.quote: Search gives you raw pages. GBrain gives you the answer.

nodes.label: Know what the brain doesn't know yet (staleness / holes)

provenance.quote: an honest note on what the brain doesn't know yet. The gap analysis is the differentiator.

nodes.label: Turn institutional knowledge into executable skills for AI automation

provenance.quote: turns it into an executable skills file for AI.

nodes.label: Query what was true at a point in time (bi-temporal fact validity)

provenance.quote: Zep + Graphiti win on bi-temporality and score 71.2% on LongMemEval versus Mem0's 49%
tracking when an event occurred and when it was ingested. Every graph edge (or relationship) includes explicit validity intervals (t_valid, t_invalid).

nodes.label: Sub-millisecond recall without LLM calls in the query path

provenance.quote: It runs on one PostgreSQL + pgvector database (no second vector store, no graph database), uses no LLM at query time

nodes.label: Context rot from stuffing full history into the window

provenance.quote: keeping the entire chat history in the model's context window (which leads to "context rot")

nodes.label: Coding agents amnesiac about everything that isn't code

provenance.quote: your coding agent stops being amnesiac about everything that isn't code.

nodes.label: Critical company know-how scattered across heads, email, Slack, tickets

provenance.quote: Every company has critical know-how scattered everywhere. Some of it lives in people's heads.

nodes.label: Per-agent memory silos: one engineer's agent learns nothing from another's

provenance.quote: When one agent discovers something important, the others never learn about it. Existing solutions are either single-machine only, require paid cloud services, or treat memory as a flat key-value store without understanding that a fact and an event are fundamentally different things.
Your engineers each run their own AI agents, but none of them can see what the others' agents learned.

nodes.label: Flat key-value memory with no distinction between facts and events

provenance.quote: treat memory as a flat key-value store without understanding that a fact and an event are fundamentally different things

nodes.label: LLM calls during graph ingestion compound cost at scale

provenance.quote: the biggest hidden cost across the field is LLM calls during ingestion: graph-construction approaches make several calls per memory add, which compounds at scale.

nodes.label: Multi-tenant org architecture

provenance.quote: Multi-tenant organizations
Every table containing customer data must include `org_id` or be clearly tenant-owned through a parent relation.

nodes.label: Org/team/project/user/agent memory scopes

provenance.quote: Org-level, team-level, project-level, user-level, and agent-level memory scopes

nodes.label: Role-based access control + per-read permission checks

provenance.quote: Role-based access control
Fine-grained permission checks before retrieval

nodes.label: Audit logs for all memory and permission events

provenance.quote: Audit logging for every memory read/write/delete/share event

nodes.label: Connectors (Slack, GitHub, Notion, Google Drive, Linear, MCP)

provenance.quote: Connector architecture for Slack, GitHub, Notion, Google Drive, Linear, and MCP servers

nodes.label: Human console + continuously-updating memory wiki

provenance.quote: One server-rendered web app — the **team mind** — where a human signs in to:

nodes.label: LLM distillation + curator synthesizing wiki pages

provenance.quote: a `CuratorWorker` synthesizes all facts + recent episodes + relations for a subject into one canonical markdown article

nodes.label: MCP-native agent memory interface

provenance.quote: MCP-native interface

nodes.label: Five memory pillars (working/semantic/episodic/procedural/strategic + work)

provenance.quote: TeamShared organizes memory across five pillars: Working, Semantic, Episodic, Procedural, and Strategic.

nodes.label: Server-side ToolCallCaptureMiddleware + /sessions/turns ingest (capture not hook-dependent)

provenance.quote: a `ToolCallCaptureMiddleware` plus the `POST /sessions/turns` ingest endpoint record turn-by-turn natural-language conversation across harnesses (gated by `capture_enabled`), so capture does not depend on Cursor-only `.ts` hooks

nodes.label: GBrain synthesis layer (gbrain think) with gap analysis

provenance.quote: the only one that does synthesis, graph traversal, and gap analysis in one box.

nodes.label: GBrain self-wiring knowledge graph (zero LLM on write)

provenance.quote: creates typed edges (`attended`, `works_at`, `invested_in`, `founded`, `advises`) with zero LLM calls.

nodes.label: GBrain autonomous dream cycle (cron enrichment overnight)

provenance.quote: Cron-driven enrichment runs while you sleep: dedup people pages, fix citations, score salience, find contradictions, prep tomorrow's tasks.

nodes.label: GBrain company brain (per-user OAuth-scoped slices, leak-tested)

provenance.quote: Each person on the team gets their own slice of the brain, scoped by login.

nodes.label: GBrain schema packs (typed page taxonomy, agent-evolvable)

provenance.quote: gbrain doesn't have a fixed layout. It ships with bundled schema packs and lets you author your own when none fit.

nodes.label: Hivemind: codify session traces into SKILL.md that propagates to team agents

provenance.quote: Hivemind doesn't just remember. It mines your team's traces for repeated patterns and codifies them into reusable skills that propagate back into every agent on the team. The agent your junior engineer used this morning is sharper because of what your senior engineer's agent figured out last week.
A background worker mines traces for repeated patterns and codifies them into `SKILL.md` files, scoped to your workspace.

nodes.label: Hivemind: every session prompt/tool-call/response captured as structured trace in Deep Lake

provenance.quote: Captures every session's prompts, tool calls, and responses as structured traces in Deeplake

nodes.label: memobase: Postgres RLS per-user isolation + OAuth + cross-tool passport

provenance.quote: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security ensures your memory is your own.
Memobase is a persistent memory layer for AI agents that leverages the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to provide shared, long-term context across disparate tools like Claude, Cursor, and Windsurf.

nodes.label: memnos: no LLM at query time + governance baked in (auth/ACL/audit/vault)

provenance.quote: uses no LLM at query time, and ships with governance — token auth, namespace ACLs, audit log, and an encrypted secret vault — in the open-source build.

nodes.label: memnos proxy: deterministic capture by relaying Anthropic/OpenAI base URLs untouched

provenance.quote: It relays every request untouched (streaming included, keys forwarded, never stored) and captures both sides of each completed exchange.

nodes.label: memory-mcp: hybrid BM25 + vector with RRF + bounded feedback rerank

provenance.quote: search_memory | Hybrid vector + BM25 search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. Supports optional, bounded feedback rerank behind a kill switch.

nodes.label: NEXO Brain: trust scoring + metacognitive guard + natural forgetting

provenance.quote: NEXO Brain — Shared brain for AI agents. Persistent memory, semantic RAG, natural forgetting, metacognitive guard, trust scoring, 150+ MCP tools.

nodes.label: multi-agent-memory: distinct fact vs event memory kinds

provenance.quote: treat memory as a flat key-value store without understanding that a fact and an event are fundamentally different things

nodes.label: xChuCx/agent-memory: git-native markdown, branch-aware, federation for cross-repo landscape stores

provenance.quote: Local, git-native project memory for AI coding agents. One MCP call in, structured memory updates out — current task state, decisions, conventions, pitfalls, per-module facts. Branch-aware. Secret-safe. Byte-preserving.
Federation lets it reference shared, git-pinned, read-only 'landscape' stores, so an agent designing a cross-service feature sees the surrounding system map.

nodes.label: xChuCx/agent-memory: durable writes stage for human review (propose → apply)

provenance.quote: durable changes stage for human review (review --diff → apply) instead of landing silently; and secrets/PII are scanned out before anything is written.

nodes.label: Graphiti: bi-temporal context graph with valid_from / invalid_at on every edge

provenance.quote: Graphiti's context graphs track how facts change over time, maintain provenance to source data
Every graph edge (or relationship) includes explicit validity intervals (t_valid, t_invalid).

nodes.label: Neo4j Agent Memory: POLE+O entities + reasoning traces (why the agent decided)

provenance.quote: reasoning memory (decision traces and tool usage audits). That reasoning layer is unique. No other tool in this comparison stores why the agent made a decision, which matters for debugging and compliance.

nodes.label: Pinecone (vector DB) / pgvector category

provenance.quote: Powered by vector databases (e.g., Pinecone) and knowledge graphs to provide rich, structured recall.

nodes.label: Neo4j context/knowledge graphs

provenance.quote: Neo4j is frequently used to build context graphs that track complex relationships between user entities and past actions.

nodes.label: LangChain / LlamaIndex memory modules

provenance.quote: LangChain and LlamaIndex offer built-in modules for both short-term conversational buffers and long-term memory RAG components.

nodes.label: Cloudflare Agent Memory (managed memory primitives)

provenance.quote: Solutions like Cloudflare Agent Memory provide developers with built-in primitives that extract, store, and manage user details across multi-session interactions automatically.

nodes.label: Hindsight-style distillation sub-agents

provenance.quote: Advanced architectures (like Hindsight) use specialized sub-agents to summarize past interactions into distilled facts and preferences

nodes.label: GBrain (garrytan/gbrain) — personal + company brain for OpenClaw/Hermes

provenance.quote: Garry Tan (YC CEO) positions GBrain as the production brain behind OpenClaw and Hermes agent deployments.
We need Garry's G-Brain, but for every business in the world.

nodes.label: Mem0 — long-term memory layer for LLM applications/agents (55k★, OpenMemory MCP branch)

provenance.quote: Mem0 is a long-term memory layer for LLM applications and AI agents that persists across/across sessions and users
Mem0 wins on speed of adoption and ecosystem (~55k GitHub stars as of May 2026).

nodes.label: Zep / Graphiti — temporal knowledge graph (bi-temporal, 71% LongMemEval)

provenance.quote: Zep takes a different structural bet. Rather than flat vector storage, it builds a temporal knowledge graph using their open-source Graphiti library.

nodes.label: Letta (ex-MemGPT) — OS-paging metaphor, self-editing memory blocks

provenance.quote: treat the LLM like an operating system managing its own memory. There's a 'main context' (RAM — what's in the prompt right now), a 'recall store' (recent conversation history), and an 'archival store' (external long-term storage).

nodes.label: Hivemind (activeloopai) — cloud-backed shared brain for Claude Code/OpenClaw/Codex/Cursor/Hermes/pi

provenance.quote: Auto-learning, cloud-backed shared brain for Claude Code • OpenClaw • Codex • Cursor • Hermes • pi agents.
The project has garnered over 1,100 GitHub stars in its first day.

nodes.label: memobase.ai — cross-tool memory passport, Postgres RLS, OAuth

provenance.quote: Memobase MCP = persistent shared memory.
PostgreSQL Row-Level Security ensures your memory is your own.

nodes.label: memnos (thameema) — self-hosted, no LLM at query, governance baked in

provenance.quote: memnos is a self-hosted memory server for AI agents. Your conversations are captured, distilled into facts, and recalled in later sessions — across Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Codex, or anything that speaks MCP, REST, or an OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible base URL.

nodes.label: memory-mcp (isaacriehm) — Postgres+pgvector, RRF hybrid search, OAuth bridge

provenance.quote: memory-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that gives AI agents durable, searchable memory backed by PostgreSQL and pgvector.

nodes.label: NEXO Brain (wazionapps) — local, trust scoring + forgetting + metacognitive guard

provenance.quote: NEXO Brain — Shared brain for AI agents. Persistent memory, semantic RAG, natural forgetting, metacognitive guard, trust scoring, 150+ MCP tools.

nodes.label: multi-agent-memory (CMPSBL) — cross-machine fact/event store for OpenClaw/Claude Code/n8n mesh

provenance.quote: Multi-Agent Memory gives your AI agents a shared brain that works across machines, tools, and frameworks. Store a fact from Claude Code on your laptop, recall it from an OpenClaw agent on your server, and get a briefing from n8n — all through the same memory system.

nodes.label: xChuCx/agent-memory — git-native markdown, branch-aware, federation

provenance.quote: Local, git-native project memory for AI coding agents. One MCP call in, structured memory updates out — current task state, decisions, conventions, pitfalls, per-module facts. Branch-aware. Secret-safe. Byte-preserving.

nodes.label: Graphiti (getzep, OSS) — bi-temporal context graphs on Neo4j/FalkorDB/Kuzu

provenance.quote: Graphiti is a framework for building and querying temporal context graphs for AI agents. Unlike static knowledge graphs, Graphiti's context graphs track how facts change over time, maintain provenance to source data

nodes.label: Neo4j Agent Memory Service — POLE+O entities + reasoning traces (55★)

provenance.quote: neo4j-agent-memory in February 2026. With 55 GitHub stars, it is the least-known tool in this comparison.

nodes.label: Headroom (chopratejas/headroom) — context compression layer, 41k★, Apache 2.0

provenance.quote: The context compression layer for AI agents
~41k GitHub stars, Apache 2.0

nodes.label: Headroom ContentRouter: per-type compression (JSON/code/logs/text/diffs)

provenance.quote: ContentRouter — detects content type, selects the right compressor

nodes.label: Headroom CCR: Compress-Cache-Retrieve reversible compression (SQLite)

provenance.quote: Unlike traditional lossy compression, CCR guarantees that every piece of original data remains accessible.

nodes.label: Headroom proxy: zero-code OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible local compression gateway

provenance.quote: headroom proxy --port 8787

nodes.label: Headroom MCP: headroom_compress / headroom_retrieve / headroom_stats

provenance.quote: MCP tools: headroom_compress, headroom_retrieve, headroom_stats.

nodes.label: Headroom is orthogonal to durable memory: shrinks current prompt, does not persist org brain

provenance.quote: Headroom: shrinks the *current prompt* (tool outputs, logs, context window) before the LLM call. TeamShared: persists *durable* semantic/episodic/procedural memory across sessions, orgs, and agents.

nodes.label: Screenpipe (screenpipe/screenpipe) — ambient desktop capture + MCP, 19k★, YC S26

provenance.quote: AI that knows what you've seen
19k+ GitHub stars, source-available Screenpipe Commercial License. Y Combinator S26.

nodes.label: Knowledge workers wanting ambient desktop recall (screen + audio)

provenance.quote: Knowledge workers who want to recall anything they've seen or heard on their computer

nodes.label: Recall anything seen or heard on the desktop without manual logging

provenance.quote: remember everything - never forget what you saw, heard, or did

nodes.label: Forgetting what you saw, heard, or did on prior days

provenance.quote: you forget what you did last tuesday - screenpipe doesn't

nodes.label: Screenpipe event-driven capture: accessibility tree + OCR fallback + audio transcription

provenance.quote: Listens for meaningful events — app switches, clicks, typing pauses, scrolling — and captures a screenshot only when something actually changes.

nodes.label: Screenpipe Pipes: trigger-based AI agents on captured data (meeting_ended, standup, CRM)

provenance.quote: Agents that act on your work, not just remember it.

nodes.label: Screenpipe MCP: query screen/audio history from Cursor and Claude Desktop

provenance.quote: claude mcp add screenpipe -- npx -y screenpipe-mcp@latest

nodes.label: Screenpipe on-device PII model: exclude-at-source + scrub cards/SSNs/keys before save

provenance.quote: scrubbed by a Screenpipe model on your machine that removes cards, SSNs, and keys before anything is saved.

nodes.label: Screenpipe Teams: per-pipe YAML data permissions enforced at OS level (fleet MDM)

provenance.quote: Enforcement at three layers — skill gating, agent interception, server middleware with per-pipe cryptographic tokens. Not prompt-based. Deterministic.

nodes.label: Screenpipe is orthogonal to org brain: ambient device capture, not shared multi-tenant memory

provenance.quote: Screenpipe: ambient OS-level ingest → local search/MCP. TeamShared: agent/human writes → org-scoped pillars → recall/think/wiki/work.

nodes.label: Agent memory splits into four pillars (working/semantic/episodic/procedural)

provenance.quote: Modern agent memory is typically divided into four distinct architectures

nodes.label: Just-in-time retrieval beats keeping full history in-context

provenance.quote: agents use pre-inference retrieval to surface only the most relevant episodic or semantic memories right when they are needed

nodes.label: YC RFS validates 'company brain' as a venture category; names GBrain as reference implementation

provenance.quote: I think every company in the world is going to need one.

nodes.label: GBrain has distribution lead: 23k stars, YC CEO author, native OpenClaw/Hermes wiring

provenance.quote: 23.4k GitHub stars, YC CEO author, named in YC RFS, native OpenClaw/Hermes integration

nodes.label: MCP is the de facto 'USB-C for memory' standard; all serious players expose memory through MCP

provenance.quote: Model Context Protocol — 'USB-C for memory' proposed by Anthropic in November 2024 — has become the de facto standard for agent ↔ external memory store communication. OpenMemory MCP from Mem0, Anthropic Memory Tool, Graphiti MCP server, Neo4j Agent Memory Service — all expose memory through MCP.

nodes.label: No memory system wins on more than 3 of 8 dimensions; each leads on a distinct axis

provenance.quote: No system wins in more than three of the eight dimensions. Mem0 wins on speed of adoption and ecosystem... Zep + Graphiti win on bi-temporality... Letta wins on self-editing memory and sleep-time compute. LangMem wins if you're already using LangGraph.

nodes.label: Zep's LongMemEval lead over Mem0 (71% vs 49%) is methodologically disputed

provenance.quote: Zep achieves 9[8].% on DMR (Deep Memory Retrieval)... 71.2% on [LongMemEval] with GPT-4o. Mem0 stops at 49%... are the subject of a methodological dispute — a corrected [calculation] indicates a score [lower] than Zep initially [reported].

nodes.label: Bi-temporal validity is the legitimate Graphiti moat for point-in-time queries; no rival matches it

provenance.quote: Graphiti's context graphs track how facts change over time, maintain provenance to source data
tracking when an event occurred and when it was ingested. Every graph edge (or relationship) includes explicit validity intervals (t_valid, t_invalid)

nodes.label: Hivemind ships the same skill-codification thesis as teamshared's skills pillar — but auto-mines from traces

provenance.quote: Hivemind doesn't just remember. It mines your team's traces for repeated patterns and codifies them into reusable skills that propagate back into every agent on the team.
A background worker mines traces for repeated patterns and codifies them into `SKILL.md` files, scoped to your workspace.

nodes.label: Multiple rivals (multi-agent-memory, memnos, teamshared MemoryKind) independently converge on distinguishing facts from events

provenance.quote: Existing solutions... treat memory as a flat key-value store without understanding that a fact and an event are fundamentally different things.

nodes.label: Evolve TeamShared from PoC to production for multi-tenant org customers

provenance.quote: Create a detailed technical plan to evolve TeamShared from PoC to production for multi-tenant org customers.

nodes.label: Teams actually want cross-agent shared memory
nodes.risk: The entire product thesis. If teams don't want a shared agent brain, every roadmap phase is built on sand.

nodes.label: Orgs will pay for 'shared agent context' as infrastructure
nodes.risk: Pricing/GTM viability; GBrain is MIT/open-source, Mem0/Zep/Letta all monetize cloud tiers ($19/$25/$249). TeamShared willingness-to-pay unvalidated.

nodes.label: Slack/GitHub/Notion/etc. connectors are the adoption lever
nodes.risk: GBrain already ingests meetings/email/tweets/voice via recipes. Connector roadmap may be table stakes, not differentiation.

nodes.label: SSO/SAML/SOC2 gate the deals worth chasing
nodes.risk: GBrain company-brain tutorial targets 10-50 person teams with OAuth — may win design partners before TeamShared enterprise phase.

nodes.label: Default cross-agent visibility (shared brain) is what users want
nodes.risk: GBrain company brain scopes per-login slices. If isolation-by-default wins, TeamShared's open recall default is a trust risk.

nodes.label: Humans will actually browse the memory wiki/console
nodes.risk: GBrain optimizes for agent-query (`gbrain think`) not human browsing. Wiki investment may not match how winners get used.

nodes.label: LLM distillation yields durable, low-noise memory worth recalling
nodes.risk: GBrain's gap analysis and eval CI gates set a higher bar for synthesis quality than raw distillation.

nodes.label: GBrain or Cognee is the primary direct competitor (not the broader field)
nodes.risk: Positioning and roadmap sequencing. Cognee (~27.5k★) matches GBrain (~23k★) as Tier-1 graph/memory platform with managed cloud; Hivemind ships auto-skill-mining; Mem0/Zep/Letta have massive adoption. GBrain-only framing misses Cognee in the same MCP slot.

nodes.label: Synthesis + gap analysis is required to win (not just hybrid recall)
nodes.risk: Product investment. TeamShared recall returns records; GBrain `think` returns answers. Missing synthesis may feel like a search engine.

nodes.label: Bi-temporal fact validity is not needed for TeamShared's buyer (timestamp-only facts suffice)
nodes.risk: If buyers want point-in-time queries ('what did Sarah own in Q1'), Graphiti/Zep win decisively on a 22-point LongMemEval gap. The curator compensates by rewriting pages but cannot answer historical queries.

nodes.label: Explicit memory_skill_set is enough; an auto-trace-to-skill miner is not needed
nodes.risk: Hivemind ships the auto-mine-from-traces loop now and pitches it as the headline differentiator. If buyers expect skills to compound automatically, TeamShared's manual skill authoring feels like a notepad vs Hivemind's database-backed brain.

nodes.label: TeamShared doesn't need a published benchmark number to compete
nodes.risk: Mem0 published ECAI 2025; Zep claims 71% LongMemEval. Without a number, the strategic KPI ('reliable enough to trust') is asserted, not proven — buyers comparing platforms have no objective recall-quality signal.

nodes.label: Cognee (topoteretes/cognee) — graph+vector AI memory platform (~27.5k★)

provenance.quote: Cognee is the open-source AI memory platform that gives AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions.

nodes.label: Cognee v1.0 remember / recall / improve / forget lifecycle

provenance.quote: query via `remember` / `recall` / `improve` / `forget`, expose through MCP, REST

nodes.label: Cognee triple-store architecture (relational + vector + graph)

provenance.quote: Cognee pitches **graph+vector knowledge infrastructure** with induced ontologies and pluggable stores

nodes.label: Cognee MCP: 14 specialized tools (remember/recall/forget preferred)

provenance.quote: **14 specialized tools** for memory management, code intelligence, data ops.

nodes.label: Cognee Cloud managed SaaS (platform.cognee.ai)

provenance.quote: Ships both OSS self-host and **Cognee Cloud** managed SaaS.

nodes.label: Cognee session cache with background improve bridge to permanent graph

provenance.quote: with `self_improvement=True` (default) bridges to permanent graph in background via `improve`.

nodes.label: Cognee recall auto-routing (summary, graph, temporal, coding-rules, lexical)

provenance.quote: Auto-routing by default: classifies query and picks retrieval strategy

nodes.label: Cognee induced ontologies during ingestion

provenance.quote: derived concepts → **induced ontologies** → searchable memory

nodes.label: Cognee ingest SKILL.md into dataset with improvement proposals

provenance.quote: Skills: `ingest-skill` (SKILL.md), list/get dataset skills, skill-improvement proposals

nodes.label: Cognee multi-user dataset-level isolation (tenants, roles, ACL)

provenance.quote: **Isolated recall**: retrieval scoped to datasets the authenticated user can read.

nodes.label: LLM calls are stateless — no memory of prior requests or documents

provenance.quote: When you call an LLM, each request is stateless: it doesn't remember what happened in the last call

nodes.label: Memory layer that links documents and carries context across LLM calls

provenance.quote: You need a memory layer that can link your documents together and create the right context for every LLM call.

nodes.label: Cognee is Tier-1 direct memory competitor (not adjacent like mex/Headroom)

provenance.quote: **Tier 1 — direct memory brain** | GBrain, Cognee, TeamShared | Primary competitor

nodes.label: Cognee and GBrain are comparable OSS scale (~27k vs ~23k stars)

provenance.quote: Cognee and GBrain are the two highest-star open-source "agent memory platform" peers in this corpus (~27k vs ~23k stars).

nodes.label: Cognee ships managed cloud SKU — closer to TeamShared GTM than GBrain OSS-only

provenance.quote: Cognee leads on graph ontology infrastructure, managed cloud SKU, and format breadth.

nodes.label: mex (mex-memory/mex) — repo-scoped markdown scaffold + drift CLI (~1.1k★)

provenance.quote: mex's category: **persistent project memory for AI coding agents** — a structured markdown scaffold in-repo plus a CLI that detects when that scaffold drifts from the real codebase.

nodes.label: mex: 11 zero-token drift checkers (paths, deps, staleness, broken links)

provenance.quote: Eleven checkers validate scaffold against codebase. **Zero tokens, zero AI.**

nodes.label: mex ROUTER.md task-type → context file routing (~120-token anchor)

provenance.quote: The agent starts with a tiny auto-loaded file. That file points to `ROUTER.md`, and the router loads only the context needed for the current task.

nodes.label: mex-mcp draft PR (#84): mex_check, mex_read_file, mex_log, mex_heartbeat

provenance.quote: Draft PR [#84](https://github.com/mex-memory/mex/pull/84) (`packages/mex-mcp`, stdio, **draft/open** as of 2026-06-18)

nodes.label: Agent instruction files drift away from the real codebase

provenance.quote: it floods the context window, burns tokens, and drifts away from the real codebase.

nodes.label: Every agent session starts cold with a prompt dump

provenance.quote: AI agents forget everything between sessions. mex gives them permanent, navigable project memory so every session starts with the right context instead of a cold prompt dump.

nodes.label: mex is Tier-3 repo-scoped adjacent — not a multi-tenant org brain

provenance.quote: Fits **Tier 3 — Git-native / repo-scoped memory** alongside xChuCx/agent-memory

nodes.label: mex addresses repo hygiene; TeamShared addresses org recall — complementary axis

provenance.quote: mex does not pitch multi-tenant org scoping, five memory pillars, distillation workers, or a human console wiki

nodes.label: Palantir Foundry ontology is architectural reference, not a memory competitor

provenance.quote: Treat as directional competitive/architectural intelligence for TeamShared ontology design, not a build spec.

nodes.label: Palantir action types: governed writes with validation, side effects, audit

provenance.quote: An action type is the schema definition of a set of changes or edits to objects, property values, and links that a user can take at once.

nodes.label: Palantir ontology: object types (nouns) + link types (relationships) + interfaces

nodes.label: TeamShared governance (work queue, OKRs, approvals, capture) differentiates vs Cognee without graph parity
